He called on readers to build an aeroplane together, but at the time the general public was not very interested in aviation.

questions.additionalText: In 1865 the first public garden was ceremonially opened in Tbilisi and was named after Alexander. It was designed by the German architect Otto Simonson, who lived and worked in Tbilisi for many years. His name is associated with numerous buildings that still adorn our capital today. One of them is the National Palace of Youth Students. Simonson specially designed exhibition pavilions, a restaurant, and fountains for the garden.
